From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league season

[Divizia B](/wiki/Divizia_B "Divizia B")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Season | 1996–97 |
| Country | [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") |
| Teams | 36 (2x18) |
| Promoted | [Foresta Fălticeni](/wiki/Foresta_F%C4%83lticeni "Foresta Fălticeni") [CSM Reșița](/wiki/CSM_Re%C8%99i%C8%9Ba "CSM Reșița") |
| Relegated | [Cetatea Târgu Neamț](/wiki/FC_Ozana_T%C3%A2rgu_Neam%C8%9B "FC Ozana Târgu Neamț") [Minaur Zlatna](/w/index.php?title=CS_Zlatna&action=edit&redlink=1 "CS Zlatna (page does not exist)") [Steaua Mizil](/w/index.php?title=Steaua_Mizil&action=edit&redlink=1 "Steaua Mizil (page does not exist)") [CFR Timișoara](/wiki/FC_CFR_Timi%C8%99oara "FC CFR Timișoara") |
| Top goalscorer | [Claudiu Niculescu](/wiki/Claudiu_Niculescu "Claudiu Niculescu") (24 goals) |
| ← [1995–96](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_Divizia_B "1995–96 Divizia B")  [1997–98](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_Divizia_B "1997–98 Divizia B") → | |

The **1996–97 Divizia B** was the 57th season of the second tier of the [Romanian football league system](/wiki/Romanian_football_league_system "Romanian football league system").

The format has been maintained to two series, each of them having 18 teams. At the end of the season, the winners of the series promoted to [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") and the last two places from both series relegated to [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Renamed teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_Divizia_B&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Renamed teams")]

*Danubiana București* merged with *Astra Ploiești* and was renamed as [Danubiana Ploiești](/wiki/FC_Astra_Giurgiu "FC Astra Giurgiu").

*Unirea Alba Iulia* was renamed as [Apulum Alba Iulia](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Alba_Iulia "FC Unirea Alba Iulia")
